root_text.an5.20:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu attahitāya ca paṭipanno hoti parahitāya ca.
root_text.an5.20:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu attanā ca sīlasampanno hoti, parañca sīlasampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an5.20:1.4: attanā ca samādhisampanno hoti, parañca samādhisampadāya samādapeti, attanā ca paññāsampanno hoti, parañca paññāsampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an5.20:1.5: attanā ca vimuttisampanno hoti, parañca vimuttisampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an5.20:1.6: attanā ca vimuttiñāṇadassanasampanno hoti, parañca vimuttiñāṇadassanasampadāya samādapeti.
root_text.an5.20:1.7: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu attahitāya ca paṭipanno hoti parahitāya cā”ti.

translation_text.an5.20:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with five qualities is practicing for both their own welfare and that of others.
translation_text.an5.20:1.3: It’s when a mendicant is personally accomplished in ethics, immersion, wisdom, freedom, and the knowledge and vision of freedom. And they encourage others in these qualities.
translation_text.an5.20:1.7: A mendicant with these five qualities is practicing both for their own welfare and that of others.”
